summary: Matched the remaining sysdolphin baselib HSD_WObj implementation by moving wobj.s out of the object list, adding a full C implementation in src/sysdolphin/baselib/wobj.c, expanding WObj/JObj/RObj/Object headers, and propagating newly identified WObj-related symbol names through existing assembly references. The PR also documented two matching-specific tactics: force-inlining JObj matrix helpers with #pragma always_inline because normal inline depth did not produce the required SetPosition codegen, and using an asm inline fallback for HSD_WObjAlloc because the generated/nonmatching C path did not match.
what_changed: Removed asm/sysdolphin/baselib/wobj.s from obj_files.mk and replaced it with a C implementation in src/sysdolphin/baselib/wobj.c.
Added HSD_WObj class data and default-class handling: hsdWObj, default_class, WObjInfoInit, WObjRelease, WObjAmnesia, HSD_WObjSetDefaultClass, HSD_WObjAlloc, and class-info setup against hsdObj.
Implemented WObj animation and descriptor paths including HSD_WObjAddAnim, WObjUpdateFunc, HSD_WObjInterpretAnim, WObjLoad, HSD_WObjInit, and HSD_WObjLoadDesc.
Implemented WObj position APIs: HSD_WObjSetPosition, HSD_WObjSetPositionX, HSD_WObjSetPositionY, HSD_WObjSetPositionZ, and HSD_WObjGetPosition.
Added WObj header declarations and macros: extern HSD_WObjInfo hsdWObj, HSD_WOBJ_INFO, HSD_WOBJ_METHOD, and prototypes for the newly matched WObj APIs.
Added always_inline JObj helpers HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty and HSD_JObjSetupMatrix in jobj.h so WObj position functions could match when updating transformed positions.
Added supporting declarations in robj.h for HSD_RObjRemoveAll, HSD_RObjResolveRefsAll, and HSD_RObjLoadDesc, plus extern HSD_ClassInfo hsdObj in object.h.

Renamed many existing assembly call targets from anonymous func_* labels to recovered names such as HSD_WObjGetPosition, HSD_WObjSetPosition, HSD_WObjAddAnim, HSD_WObjInterpretAnim, HSD_WObjInit, HSD_WObjAlloc, splArcLengthPoint, and hsdIsDescendantOf.
Added a Makefile rule forcing src/sysdolphin/baselib/wobj.c.o to use CC_EPI := $(CC), consistent with other matched baselib C objects needing the main compiler path.
smart_moves: Used #pragma always_inline around small JObj matrix helpers because the PR author observed normal inlining respected depth limits and broke matching for SetPosition functions.
Kept a NON_MATCHING C version of HSD_WObjAlloc for readability while providing an asm implementation in the matching path, preserving both documentation of intended logic and exact codegen.
Propagated symbol names into unrelated remaining-asm files after matching WObj, improving searchability and future decomp context for CObj, LObj, JObj, stage code, and generic text assembly.
Centralized WObj class behavior through HSD_WOBJ_METHOD/HSD_WOBJ_INFO and class init hooks instead of treating descriptor loading/allocation as standalone helpers.
Handled animation callback type cases explicitly: type 4 clamps spline progress to 0.0-1.0, samples splArcLengthPoint through a JObj spline, and types 5-7 drive X/Y/Z position setters.
decomp_lessons: For small helper functions that must appear inside a caller, ordinary inline may be insufficient under this compiler; #pragma always_inline can be necessary to overcome inline-depth behavior.
When a function will not match cleanly in C, an asm inline guarded by NON_MATCHING can be a pragmatic bridge: retain the readable C intent while keeping the build matched.
Matching one baselib object often requires exposing adjacent subsystem declarations in headers; here WObj needed JObj matrix helpers, RObj load/resolve/remove declarations, and hsdObj class info.
Recovered names should be applied to existing assembly callsites immediately, because it documents call relationships and helps future PRs identify dependencies.

Position getters/setters may include lazy transform resolution: WObj checks flag bit 1, optionally calls HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, transforms with PSMTXMUltiVec, clears the flag, then updates or returns pos.
Animation update callbacks may need exact assert strings and line numbers for matching; this PR preserved wobj.c assert file strings and evidence of line-number sensitivity.
naming_conventions: Public WObj APIs use HSD_WObj* CamelCase names, e.g. HSD_WObjAddAnim, HSD_WObjInterpretAnim, HSD_WObjInit, HSD_WObjLoadDesc, HSD_WObjSetPosition, and HSD_WObjGetPosition.
Static WObj internals use WObj* names without the HSD_ prefix, e.g. WObjUpdateFunc, WObjLoad, WObjRelease, WObjAmnesia, and WObjInfoInit.
Class macros follow baselib style: HSD_WOBJ_INFO(i) casts to HSD_WObjInfo*, and HSD_WOBJ_METHOD(o) accesses HSD_OBJECT_METHOD(o) as WObj info.
Recovered non-WObj baselib names include hsdIsDescendantOf and splArcLengthPoint, replacing generic func_80382538 and func_803792C8 labels at callsites.
The default WObj class static is named default_class and was reflected in include/variables.h as a recovered variable name rather than a lbl_ symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added $(EPILOGUE_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/wobj.c.o: CC_EPI := $(CC) in Makefile, suggesting this object needed the primary compiler assignment used by other matched files.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/wobj.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES once wobj.c supplied the matched implementation.
Wrapped HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty and HSD_JObjSetupMatrix with #pragma push, #pragma always_inline on, and #pragma pop to force exact inlining into WObj position functions.
Used #ifdef NON_MATCHING for the readable C HSD_WObjAlloc and an asm HSD_WObjAlloc in the matching path with nofralloc and exact instruction sequence.
Used #pragma peephole on around HSD_WObjAlloc's NON_MATCHING C section and around WObjRelease/WObjAmnesia/WObjInfoInit, indicating peephole behavior mattered for local codegen.

Renamed branch targets in remaining assembly without changing instructions, e.g. func_8037D720 to HSD_WObjGetPosition and func_8037D45C to HSD_WObjSetPosition, preserving assembly while improving symbol knowledge.
review_feedback: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body itself notes two author-discovered matching constraints: JObj.h inlines needed always_inline, and one function was asm-inlined because the normal tooling/C output did not match.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the asm inline for HSD_WObjAlloc or convert the NON_MATCHING path to matching C.
Search for subsequent cleanup of WObj assert line numbers and string labels such as lbl_804D5EF8 and lbl_804D5F04.
Look for later PRs that decompile CObj/LObj callsites now that HSD_WObjGetPosition, HSD_WObjSetPosition, and HSD_WObjInterpretAnim were named.
Check whether HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty and HSD_JObjSetupMatrix remain forced always_inline or are later moved/refactored.
Search for PSMTXMUltiVec typo/casing usage around WObj position transforms in later code, since this PR's diff shows that exact symbol in WObj setters/getter.
